description: People who share a craft or profession may form a continuing association called a 'temu'. They can practise together and govern shared resources. Some guilds teach or maintain standards; others represent the work in public. The word prescribes no internal rank and grants no authority beyond what the association actually holds.
articulatory_notes: Touch the tongue tip to the backs of the upper front teeth for unvoiced dental 't' and release into stressed middle-height front 'e'. Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm' and finish with high-back rounded 'u'.
sound_symbolism: A light dental closure opens into 'e', then the lips come together for 'm' before rounded 'u'. The compact movement from one contact to another can suit a small body gathered around shared work.
usage_notes: 'temu' is the organisation itself. In a possessive phrase, the craft may come first, but the noun alone never implies membership. 'shorai' (commune) is organised around shared life and resources; 'temu' is organised around a craft or profession.

pillars.solarpunk-values: A guild can keep costly tools in common and knowledge in circulation. It can also give practitioners a voice in decisions about their work.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Historical guilds preserved techniques and mutual support, but some also restricted entry or protected privilege. The Phi word covers the association without idealising its conduct.

semantic_domains.community: an association formed around shared skilled practice
semantic_domains.creation: organisation of craft knowledge, tools, and standards
